content.problemStatement:
Given a matrix sized m x n, we define an O-region as a non-empty sub-block with another non-empty sub-block inside removed, such that the removed sub-block has no common boundaries with the outside.  That is, the leftmost column of the removed sub-block must be different from the leftmost column of the outer sub-block, and similarly the rightmost columns, topmost rows, and bottommost rows are different. The removal of the inner sub-block leaves a region which looks like the letter O.


The value of an O-region is the sum of all numbers in the outer sub-block but not in the removed one. Your task is finding the maximal value of all O-regions appearing in the given matrix.
constraints: Absolute value of each integer in the matrix is at most 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two positive integers m and n, representing the dimensions of the matrix. m lines follow, each containing n integers describing the matrix.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximal value of all O-regions appearing in the given matrix.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maximum O-region value is 6, which can be obtained by removing the inner sub-block from the outer sub-block.

solution.approach: The key insight is to calculate the sum of all sub-matrices and then find the maximum O-region value by subtracting the sum of the inner sub-matrix from the sum of the outer sub-matrix. We use a brute force approach to check all possible O-regions.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * m^4 * n^4) per test case, O(T * m^4 * n^4) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(m * n) — the dominant structure is the input matrix.
